methods.name: translate
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a new translation matrix created from tx, ty and tz. The translation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if T is the new translation matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = T * M.

methods.name: rotate
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by 3 rotation matrices, first a rotation around the X axis by rx degrees, followed by a rotation around the Y axis by ry degrees, followed by the same for rz. The rotation values are in degrees. The rotation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. So if M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = RZ * RY * RX * M.

methods.name: rotateOnAxis
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a new rotation matrix created by rotation angle degrees around the axis specified by rotationAxis. The angle is in degrees. The rotation is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if R is the new rotation matrix specified by rotationAxis and angle, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = R * M.

methods.name: scale
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a scale matrix created from sx, sy and sz. The scale is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if S is the new scale matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = S * M.

methods.name: lookat
methods.description: Multiplies the current matrix by a lookat matrix created using the given values to the matrix. The lookat matrix is applied from the left of the matrix by default. That is to say, if L is the new lookat matrix, and M is the current matrix, then the result of this operation is M = L * M. The values for to parameters can be given as anything that can be treated as a list of 3 values. E.g a TDU.Vector, TDU.Position or simply a list of size 3.

methods.name: decompose
methods.description: Decomposes the matrix into its scale, rotate and translate values. These are the same as the translate, rotate and scale that are in the

methods.name: projectionFrustum
methods.signature: projectionFrustum(left, right, bottom, top, near, far)
methods.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with a projection matrix using the given frustum extents. The left, right, bottom, top extents are located on the near plane. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan.

methods.name: projectionFovX
methods.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with a projection matrix defined by the FOV(given in degrees), an aspect ratio and near/far planes. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan.

methods.name: projectAndClip
methods.description: Applies a projection matrix to a position if it's within the normalized clip space. Returns None if projected position is clipped.

methods.name: projectionStereo
methods.signature: projectionStereo(ipd : float, convergeZ : float, fovX : float, aspectX : float, aspectY : float, near : float, far : float, rightEye : bool=False)
methods.description: Replaces the contents of the matrix with an asymetrical projection matrix suitable for stereo rendering. The left eye's projection matrix is given by default, set rightEye=True to get the right eye's instead. For proper rendering, the cameras will also need to be translated in X by -ipd/2 and +ipd/2 for the left and right eyes respectively. The depth range generated by this matrix will be [0,1] from near to far, as is required by Vulkan.

methods.name: rand
methods.description: Return a random value in the range [0.0, 1.0) given the input seed value. That is, it will never return 1.0, but it may return 0.0. For a given seed, it will always return the same random number. The seed does not need to be a number. If the seed is not numeric, it resolves it to its string representation to produce a unique value. In the case of OPs for example, its string representation is a constant path. Thus one can produce a unique random value for each OP which remains the same for that OP each time you reload TouchDesigner.

methods.name: startBlock
methods.description: Start a named undo block. While the block is active, any script changes to TouchDesigner objects that are undoable in the editor will be added to the undo stack.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Clear undo and redo stack. This will terminate any current undo blocks.

methods.name: addCallback
methods.description: Add a Python callback into the current undo block.

methods.name: redo
methods.description: Redo the next operation. This will terminate any current undo blocks.

methods.name: undo
methods.description: Undo the last operation. This will terminate any current undo blocks.

members.name: x
members.description: Returns a new vector with all 3 components being negated.

members.name: y
members.description: Gets or sets the Y component of the vector.

members.name: z
members.description: Gets or sets the Z component of the vector.

methods.name: angle
methods.description: Returns the angel (in degrees) between the current vector and specified vector (vec).

methods.name: scale
methods.description: Scales each component of the vector by the specified values.

methods.name: normalize
methods.description: Makes the length of this vector 1.

methods.name: length
methods.description: Returns the length of this vector.

methods.name: lengthSquared
methods.description: Returns the squared length of this vector.

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Returns a new vector that is a copy of the vector.

methods.name: distance
methods.description: Returns the distance of the current vector to specified vector (vec).

methods.name: lerp
methods.description: Returns the linear interpolation of this vector and vec2. That is vec1 * (1.0 - t) + vec2 * t, where vec1 is the current vector. The value for t is not restricted to the range [0, 1].

methods.name: slerp
methods.description: Returns the spherical interpolation of this vector and vec2. The value for t is not restricted to the range [0, 1].

methods.name: dot
methods.description: Returns the dot product of this vector and the passed vector.

methods.name: cross
methods.description: Returns the cross product of this vector and the passed vector. The operation is self cross vec.

methods.name: project
methods.description: Projects this vector onto the plan defined by vec1 and vec2. Both vec1 and vec2 must be normalized. The result may not be normalized.

methods.name: reflect
methods.description: Reflects the current vector about the specified vector (vec).
